successCriteria.description: src/modules/status/status.routes.ts exists and exports a named async function statusRoutes(fastify: FastifyInstance): Promise<void> that registers GET /status
src/modules/status/index.ts re-exports SystemStatus, IStatusService, StatusService, and statusRoutes
GET /status returns HTTP 200 with JSON body { up: true, version: '1' } when the service succeeds
The route handler catches errors and returns HTTP 500 with { up: false, version: '1' } on service failure
Jest test suite at src/modules/status/__tests__/status.routes.test.ts passes, covering both success and error paths

constraints: Error handling: All async errors are caught and handled — the route handler wraps the service call in try/catch and returns a 500 with a safe fallback response
No sensitive data in logs: The status response contains only { up, version } — no PII, tokens, or secrets are logged or exposed
Repository pattern: Not applicable — this is a read-only health-check endpoint with no database access
Audit records: Not applicable — no state-changing operations occur
Input validation: Not applicable — GET /status has no request body or query parameters to validate
RBAC enforcement: Not applicable — the health-check endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated
outOfScope: Phase 1 files (src/modules/status/status.model.ts, status.service.interface.ts, status.service.ts) — already exist and are not modified
Any database schema, migrations, or repository implementations
Any other modules (leave, balance, employee, policy, notification, audit)
Authentication or authorization middleware on the /status route
Any frontend or mobile client changes
